name: HoorayHR - API Documentation
description: This is the documentation for the HoorayHR API, which can be used by partners and admins to interact with the system. ### Authentication HoorayHR supports two authentication methods: **OAuth** and **Personal API Keys**. #### Personal API Keys for admins Intended for internal use within a company a personal API key will allow you to interact with the system as the user who created the key. ##### Pre-requisites - Requires the user to have the `admin` role within the company ##### Step 1. Obtaining a personal API key 1. Navigate to the [API Keys](https://app.hoorayhr.io/settings/api-keys) page - If this page doesn't load for you, please ensure the pre-requisites are met. 2. Obtain a personal API key by clicking the "New API Key" button and filling in the fields. - `Name`: the name of the API key - `Expiration date`: the date the API key will expire (recommended) - `Scopes`: the scopes needed for the intent of the API key **Note:** After creating the API key, you cannot edit its scopes or expiration date. The key will be shown only once, please copy and __store it securely.__ ##### Step 2. Making requests with the personal API key Use the personal API key to make requests to the API by including it in the `Authorization` header as `Bearer ` - The `api_key` is always prefixed with `pk_` #### OAuth for partners Intended for partners. OAuth allows partners to create applications that can access the HoorayHR API on behalf of users. ##### Pre-requisites production - Requires OAuth client information (provided by HoorayHR) - `client_id`: Your client ID - `client_secret`: Your client secret - `redirect_uri`: Your redirect URI ##### Pre-requisites development - You can use a test OAuth client ID and secret for development purposes. Note: this client only works on the preview environment. - `api url`: https://api.preview.hoorayhr.io - `app url`: https://preview.hoorayhr.io - `client_id`: `test_client` - `client_secret`: `test_client` - `redirect_uri`: Your redirect URI ##### Step 1. Obtaining client ID and secret You can obtain a client ID and secret by contacting us at [support@hoorayhr.io](mailto:support@hoorayhr.io) or by using the test client information provided above. ##### Step 2. Retrieving the authorization code Obtain an OAuth code by redirecting the user to the front-end `/oauth/authorize` endpoint (`https://app.hoorayhr.io/oauth/authorize`) with the following parameters: - `response_type`: `code` - `client_id`: Your client ID - `redirect_uri`: Your redirect URI - `scope`: The scopes you want to request as a space-separated list **Structure:** `https://app.hoorayhr.io/oauth/authorize?response_type=code&client_id=&redirect_uri=&scope=` **Example:** `https://app.hoorayhr.io/oauth/authorize?response_type=code&client_id=123123123123123123123123&redirect_uri=https:%2F%2Fexample.com&scope=profile_read%20profile_write` ##### Step 3. HoorayHR requests consent from the user A prompt will appear to the user asking for permission to access the requested scopes. The user can either accept or deny the request. After the user has made a choice, they will be redirected back to your redirect URI with a code in the query string. ##### Step 4. Exchanging the authorization code for an access token Obtain an access token by sending a POST request to the `/oauth/token` endpoint (`https://api.hoorayhr.io/oauth/token`) with the following parameters: - `grant_type`: `authorization_code` - `code`: The code you obtained (in step 3) - `client_id`: Your client ID - `client_secret`: Your client secret - `redirect_uri`: Your redirect URI (must match the one used in step 2) **Structure:** `https://api.hoorayhr.io/oauth/token/?grant_type=authorization_code&code=&client_id=&client_secret=&redirect_uri=` ##### Step 5. Making requests with the access token Use the `access_token` (from step 4) to make requests to the API by including it in the `Authorization` header as `Bearer ` ### Filtering The following options are available on all* endpoints for filtering: - $in - $gt (greater than) - $gte (greater than or equal to) - $lt (less than) - $lte (less than or equal to) To use the $gt, $gte, $lt or $lte options, pass them in the query as following: `?start[$gte]=2023-01-01`. This example will only return items where the start date is greater than or equal to 2023-01-01. These options can also be used to filter numbers such as `?percentage[$lte]=60`. It is also possible to filter items using the ISO 8601 datetime format: `?createdAt[$gte]=2023-05-10T12:00:00Z&createdAt[$lte]=2023-05-10T15:00:00Z` Please check that the dates that are sent are valid dates. For example when sending 2023-02-29, the result will be empty. The $in operator is used to specify multiple values in the where clause, like: `?id[$in]=52&id[$in]=54`. * The `users` endpoints only support filtering with `$in` on the `id` field. ### Inactive companies When a company is inactive, all endpoints will return a 403 Forbidden error. The response body always contains the following JSON (alongside other properties): ```json { "code": 403, "data": { "errorCode": "INACTIVE_COMPANY" } } ``` ### Companies with overdue payments When a company's payment is overdue, all endpoints will return a 403 Forbidden error. The response body always contains the following JSON (alongside other properties): ```json { "code": 403, "data": { "errorCode": "PAYMENT_OVERDUE" } } ```
